heading: GCP Private Service Connect
body:
When you enable this feature, Google Cloud and Atlas create
the following resources to support secure connections over
VPC (Virtual Private Cloud)s:

| Resource                 | Creator                         | Description                                                                                                                                                                      |
| ------------------------ | ------------------------------- | -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| Private endpoint service | Atlas                           | A collection of private endpoint resources within your Atlas VPC (Virtual Private Cloud) that places clusters within a region behind network load balancers.                     |
| Private endpoints        | Google Cloud and Atlas together | A generic term for the private connections established between Atlas and your cloud provider. For Google Cloud, those connections are established using Private Service Connect. |

When you enable GCP Private Service Connect in Google Cloud, Atlas
creates a private endpoint service using a [service attachment](https://cloud.google.com/vpc/docs/private-service-connect#service-attachments)
and a load balancer.

Next, you create resources that establish a one-way
connection from your VPC (Virtual Private Cloud) to the private endpoint service
in Atlas using a private endpoint. The private endpoint
service routes traffic to the load balancer for the
clusters in the Atlas VPC (Virtual Private Cloud).

One Cluster, One Region

The following diagram shows how GCP Private Service Connect
establishes connections when you have one cluster
in one region.

![Image showing how GCP Private Service Connect establishes connections from your application VPC to resources in the |service| VPC when you have one cluster in one region.](/images/google-psc-diagram-new.svg)

Multiple Clusters, One Region

The following diagram shows how GCP Private Service Connect
establishes connections when you have two
clusters in one region.

![Image showing how GCP Private Service Connect establishes connections from your application VPC to resources in the |service| VPC when you have two clusters in one region.](/images/google-psc-diagram-two-clusters-new.svg)

One Cluster, Multiple Regions

The following diagram shows how GCP Private Service Connect
establishes connections when you have one cluster
in multiple regions.

![Image showing how GCP Private Service Connect establishes connections from your application VPC to resources in the |service| VPC when you have one cluster in multiple regions.](/images/google-psc-diagram-multiple-regions-new.svg)
